summary:
Claim: Giant cell arteritis does not typically cause palpable purpura.  

Evidence: - Palpable purpura results from small-vessel vasculitis; GCA involves large-artery inflammation. - Conditions like Henoch-Schönlein purpura (HSP), mixed cryoglobulinemia, and drug-induced vasculitis commonly present with palpable purpura due to immune-mediated microangiopathy.  

Answer: C (Giant Cell Arteritis) ✅

(498 tokens)
